repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
This PR adds the ability to create custom interactive submodes:

- Repeatedly ask user for key presses via `getcharstr()`.
- Handle each key with a dedicated function handler that acts on a persistent state table. It is allowed to change Vim modes (that's why this is a "submode" and not a full mode), text, cursor, etc. This also by default does not change Vim mode while interacting with the user, which simplifies *a lot* of things: like in Visual and Operator-pending modes.
- Stop when handle says so or after `<C-c>`. Return a value that was set by the handler. This allows writing code that interacts with the user but is still executed synchronously.

---

This is my take on #992. This overall approach is used in many 'mini.nvim' modules and I somewhat recently realized that this can be neatly generalized for upstrem. Examples:

- 'mini.pick' picker with `vim.ui.select`.
- 'mini.input' user input with `vim.ui.input`.
- 'mini.clue' with custom "trigger submode" to show clues while navigating mappings.
- 'mini.align' to have interactive alignment.
- 'mini.jump2d' to show labels and interactively filter them.
- 'mini.ai', 'mini.surround', 'mini.files' have small "one key press" submodes that only get a single key from the user, but also fits this design.

---

How it can be useful for core functionality (outside of providing tools for custom submodes):

- More advanced and flexible alternative to `vim.fn.confirm`.
- Synchronous `vim.ui.input`. Either default or a complete rewrite as a submode with user-customizable view and key handlers. Related to #39719.
- Synchronous `vim.ui.select`. Either default or a complete rewrite as a submode with user-customizable view, key, preview, match handlers. Which I very much think is the good approach to take.

---

This is a mostly ready PoC. If this general approach is accepted, there is work remaining:

- Add tests.
- Allow "hooking" into submode:
  - `SubmodeEnter`, `SubmodeLeave`, `SubmodeChanged` events.
  - `opts.desc_keys` to allow submode "advertise" what it can do. Helpful for "which-key" like plugins.
  - `vim.submode.set(state)` to set state from the outside. Allows adjusting handler, options, etc.
- Make implementing submodes more streamlined (as seen in the next comment with demos):
  - Enhance `getcharstr` to not block redraw will make submodes much easier. It would also help if it would show cursor at its up to date place.
  - A way to perform text manipulation based on supplied keys would also simplify things. Like `vim.text.as_cmdline(text, caret, keys) -> { text=new_text, caret=new_caret }`. So then `vim.text.as_cmdline('abcd', 3, { '\21' })` returns `{ text='cd', caret=1 }`.

comment_regular_data.body:
Here is a showcase of what is possible with this PR's approach and the full code needed for it:

### Window manipulation submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Window manipulation
_G.cmd_window = function()
  local handler = function(state, key)
    if not (state.status == 'progress' and key ~= nil) then return end
    if key == '\r' or key == '\27' then state.status = 'end' end
    pcall(vim.cmd.wincmd, key)
    vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })
  end
  return vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Window', getchar_opts = { cursor = 'hide' } }).status
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d46b9dc7-e54f-4e3e-a83c-10cbaf2f8d18

</details>

### Move line submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Move line
_G.move_line = function()
  local move_cmd = { j = 'move +1', k = 'move -2', h = 'normal! <<', l = 'normal! >>' }
  local handler = function(state, key)
    if not (state.status == 'progress' and key ~= nil) then return end
    if move_cmd[key] then
      vim.cmd(move_cmd[key])
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })
    else
      state.status = 'end'
      vim.api.nvim_feedkeys(key, 't', false)
    end
  end
  return vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Move line', getchar_opts = { cursor = 'hide' } }).status
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b6c40c7-e862-4a44-ab69-63fe7f41cbca

</details>

### Confirm submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Confirm
_G.confirm = function(msg, choices)
  local choices_list = {}
  for k, v in vim.spairs(choices) do
    choices_list[#choices_list + 1] = k .. '=' .. v
  end
  local choices_str = table.concat(choices_list, ', ')

  local handler = function(state, key)
    if state.status == 'start' then
      vim.api.nvim_echo({ { msg, 'Question' }, { '\n' }, { choices_str, 'WarningMsg' } }, false, {})
    elseif state.status == 'progress' and choices[key] ~= nil then
      state.output = choices[key]
      state.status = 'end'
    elseif state.status == 'end' or state.status == 'cancel' then
      vim.cmd.mode()
    end
  end
  local res = vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Confirm' })
  return res.status == 'end' and res.output or nil
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42926b85-6f7d-44b0-a665-b052618c935f

</details>

### Tree-sitter incremental selection submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Incremental selection
_G.ts_select = function()
  local targets = { i = 'parent', I = 'child', n = 'next', N = 'prev', e = 'extend_next', E = 'extend_prev' }
  local handler = function(state, key)
    if state.status == 'start' then
      vim.treesitter.select('parent')
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })
    elseif state.status == 'progress' then
      if targets[key] then vim.treesitter.select(targets[key]) end
      if key == '\r' then state.status = 'end' end
      if key == '\27' then state.status = 'cancel' end
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })
    elseif state.status == 'end' or state.status == 'cancel' then
      if vim.fn.mode():find('[vV\22]') then vim.cmd('normal! ' .. vim.fn.mode()) end
    end
  end
  return vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Select incremental', getchar_opts = { cursor = 'keep' } }).status
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a2d2e78c-cc62-48ba-be08-d2f356d7aef4

</details>

### User input submode with nested digraph submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Digraph
_G.digraph = function()
  local handler = function(state, key)
    if key == '\27' then
      state.status = 'cancel'
    elseif key ~= nil then
      state.data.chars = (state.data.chars or '') .. key
      if vim.fn.strchars(state.data.chars) < 2 then
        vim.api.nvim_echo({ { state.data.chars, 'Question' } }, false, {})
      else
        local ok, output = pcall(vim.fn.digraph_get, state.data.chars)
        state.output = ok and output or nil
        state.status = 'end'
      end
    elseif state.status == 'end' or state.status == 'cancel' then
      vim.cmd.mode()
    end
  end
  return vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Digraph' }).output
end

-- Simple user input (only append characters) via virtual line
_G.input = function(prompt, default)
  local handler = function(state, key)
    -- Setup by showing in virtual line below
    if state.status == 'start' then
      state.output = ''

      local ns_id = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace('Input')
      local chunks = { { prompt or 'Prompt', 'DiagnosticWarn' }, { ' ' }, { '' }, { '▏' } }
      local extmark_opts = { virt_lines = { chunks } }
      local cur = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(0)
      extmark_opts.id = vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(0, ns_id, cur[1] - 1, cur[2], extmark_opts)
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })

      state.data = { ns_id = ns_id, ex_opts = extmark_opts }

    -- Accept on <CR> and cancel on <Esc>
    elseif key == '\r' then
      state.status = 'end'
    elseif key == '\27' then
      state.status = 'cancel'

    -- Update input and virtual line
    elseif state.status == 'progress' then
      -- NOTE: NESTED SUBMODE
      if key == vim.keycode('<C-k>') then key = digraph() or '' end
      state.output = state.output .. key

      state.data.ex_opts.virt_lines[1][3] = { state.output }
      local ex_data = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_extmark_by_id(0, state.data.ns_id, state.data.ex_opts.id, {})
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(0, state.data.ns_id, ex_data[1], ex_data[2], state.data.ex_opts)
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })

    -- Cleanup
    elseif state.status == 'end' or state.status == 'cancel' then
      vim.api.nvim_buf_del_extmark(0, state.data.ns_id, state.data.ex_opts.id)
    end
  end

  local res = vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Input', init_keys = { default } })
  return res.status == 'end' and res.output or nil
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cf7eb8ba-98ec-4e3d-9bec-b9b81392e3a3

</details>

### Picker submode

<details><summary>Code</summary>

```lua
-- Simple string picker (only append characters)
_G.pick = function(items, prompt)
  local handler = function(state, key)
    -- Setup by showing in floating window
    if state.status == 'start' then
      if items == nil or #items == 0 then
        state.status = 'end'
        return
      end

      state.query = ''

      state.data.buf_id = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true)
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(state.data.buf_id, 0, -1, false, items)

      local config = { relative = 'editor', row = 0, col = 0 }
      config.height = math.min(#items, math.floor(0.5 * vim.o.lines))
      config.width = math.floor(0.382 * vim.o.columns)
      config.title = '▏'
      config.footer = prompt or 'Select'
      config.border = 'single'
      state.data.win_id = vim.api.nvim_open_win(state.data.buf_id, false, config)
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })

    -- Accept on <CR> and cancel on <Esc>
    elseif key == '\r' then
      state.output = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(state.data.buf_id, 0, 1, false)[1]
      state.output = state.output ~= '' and state.output or nil
      state.status = 'end'
    elseif key == '\27' then
      state.status = 'cancel'

    -- Match and update floating window
    elseif state.status == 'progress' then
      state.query = state.query .. key
      local lines = vim.fn.matchfuzzy(items, state.query)
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(state.data.buf_id, 0, -1, false, lines)

      local config = vim.api.nvim_win_get_config(state.data.win_id)
      config.height = math.min(math.max(#lines, 1), math.floor(0.5 * vim.o.lines))
      config.title = state.query .. '▏'
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_config(state.data.win_id, config)
      vim.cmd.redraw({ bang = true })

    -- Cleanup
    elseif state.status == 'end' or state.status == 'cancel' then
      vim.api.nvim_win_close(state.data.win_id, true)
      vim.api.nvim_buf_delete(state.data.buf_id, { force = true })
    end
  end

  local res = vim.submode.start(handler, { desc = 'Select' })
  return res.status == 'end' and res.output or nil
end
```

</details>

<details><summary>Demo</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9183ba86-7cda-4678-bd92-9592bdb34b08

</details>

> * Synchronous `vim.ui.input`. Either default or a complete rewrite as a submode with user-customizable view and key handlers. Related to [vim.ui.input synchronous ("blocking") #39719](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39719).
> * Synchronous `vim.ui.select`

interesting.

I do have the usual drive-by question about how this interacts with the rest of the editor, since the current impl looks totally disconnected from the rest of the editor). E.g. does it work with macros (that's a key benefit of "synchronous" `vim.ui.input` etc).

> E.g. does it work with macros (that's a key benefit of "synchronous" `vim.ui.input` etc).

Depends on what you mean here and general approach, I guess.

For example, with `_G.input` from [this comment](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40275#issuecomment-4709309739), executing `:imap <M-m> <Cmd>lua vim.fn.append('.', input())<CR>` mapping seems to work:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42466177-c518-414a-9600-bbc0caa92e06

why don't we just call it `vim.mode`? that is also what i [hinted at](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/992#issuecomment-2577779050).

btw, it doesn't look like this uses namespaces, which my proposal suggested because that would allow `:umap <ns>` and stuff like that.

The name "submode", and the approach currently in this PR, signals to me that shortcuts are being taken and we still don't actually have user-defined modes. Rather it's an interesting utility, but doesn't really address #992 and is not part of the long-term vision.

> why don't we just call it `vim.mode`? that is also what i [hinted at](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/992#issuecomment-2577779050).

The reason is described in the initial comment:

> Handle each key with a dedicated function handler that acts on a persistent state table. It is allowed to change Vim modes (that's why this is a "submode" and not a full mode), text, cursor, etc. This also by default does not change Vim mode while interacting with the user, which simplifies a lot of things: like in Visual and Operator-pending modes.

This PR's approach is a way to interact with the user while having the flexibility of decide how to interact with the editor state. I.e. it can not change "regular" mode or intentionally change it.

For example, if user modes are full new separate Vim mode, then working when called in Visual,  Operator-pending, and sometimes Insert modes becomes very annoying very quickly. Like, I am not sure how incremental visual selection can be made as a full separate mode.

> btw, it doesn't look like this uses namespaces, which my proposal suggested because that would allow `:umap <ns>` and stuff like that.

Because there is no need for it. This allows plugins to make functions that interact with the user and with the editor the way they want. Which is basically a separate mode.

> The name "submode", and the approach currently in this PR, signals to me that shortcuts are being taken and we still don't actually have user-defined modes. Rather it's an interesting utility, but doesn't really address #992 and is not part of the long-term vision.

If "submode" hints at "cutting corners", then let's call it "supermode"? One of the goals of this PR is to provide a new input to the long-term vision outlined in #992. The approach this PR takes proved to be very powerful and flexible with evidence of several plugins. And its generalization here is rather concise with less of a maintenance burden, which I thought was also nice towards long-term vision.

> I.e. it can not change "regular" mode or intentionally change it. ... For example, if user modes are full new separate Vim mode, then working when called in Visual, Operator-pending, and sometimes Insert modes becomes very annoying very quickly

My [proposal](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/992#issuecomment-2577779050) addresses that via "nesting", so submodes "inherit" the parent mode.

> And its generalization here is rather concise with less of a maintenance burden, which I thought was also nice towards long-term vision

Very true. OTOH, if we aren't addressing the root issue then the root issue still exists and isn't solved.

> OTOH, if we aren't addressing the root issue then the root issue still exists and isn't solved.

I am not sure what is the unaddressed root issue here. Is it not being able to do `:umap` or not using namespaces?

Currently this provides a way to interact with the user+editor with full customizability of what keys mean during it. I would say that this describes a mode. It is not called *exactly* that to not confuse with Normal, Insert, etc. modes, as it works alongside them (which is battle tested *for years* as something practically useful). The synchronous execution, being able to return a value, and concise implementation are very nice bonuses.

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: feat(submode): add user submodes
